description: During a dual-write window one of the two stores silently falls out of sync, so the new store holds incomplete or stale data at cutover.
body: Failure mode: writes go to both old and new stores but a failure, race, or missed code path updates only one, so the stores diverge and cutover serves wrong data. Detection signals: a reconciliation job reports mismatches; row counts differ between stores; some mutation paths write to only one store. Do not cut reads over to the new store while reconciliation shows drift. Safer fallback: make dual-writes transactional or idempotent with retries, alert on reconciliation divergence, fix the missed write paths, and only cut over after a clean parity window; keep old-store reads as the rollback.
